# Plan — Add OCR / Image-Text Extraction to GhostPen

*Generated by planner agent on 2026-07-17*

> **SUPERSEDED — implement from [`architecture.md`](./architecture.md) in this folder.**
> The architect review found blocking issues (trigger-flow ordering, raw bytes over IPC,
> data-URI double-prefix, unused model override, Wayland image self-read, behavior
> regression in `process_inner`). The corrected, approved plan is `architecture.md`;
> where the two disagree, the architecture wins.

## Overview

Extend GhostPen so the action menu works when the clipboard contains an **image** instead of text. The flow is: copy an image (or trigger GhostPen while the clipboard already holds one) → the menu previews the image and offers **Extract Text** → OCR runs through the existing OpenAI-compatible `/chat/completions` backend using a vision-capable model (e.g. `gemma4:e4b` multimodal) → the extracted text becomes the new working selection, so the user can immediately run any normal action (proofread, translate, etc.) or close the menu and paste the text.

This requires:

1. Upgrading the **Platform Abstraction Layer** to read and restore image clipboard data.
2. Changing the **clipboard contract** to snapshot and restore non-text payloads (image bytes), not just strings.
3. Teaching the **AI client** to send multimodal payloads (text + base64 image).
4. Surfacing image content in the **React frontend** and adding an extraction affordance.
5. Adding a small **OCR settings** panel.

The feature intentionally reuses the existing AI backend and active profile rather than adding a local OCR engine, keeping the architecture consistent with the rest of the app.

---

## Architecture Decisions

- **Multimodal LLM as the OCR engine.** GhostPen already talks to any OpenAI-compatible endpoint. Vision-capable models such as `gemma4:e4b` can extract text from images, so the feature is built on that same path. No new OCR library (Tesseract, etc.) is introduced. This keeps the provider/model configurable, but it also means the active model must be vision-capable; otherwise the action degrades with a readable error.
- **Image is a first-class "selection" type.** The clipboard is read into a new `SelectionContent` enum (`Text` | `Image`) stored in `AppState`. The rest of the menu/AI flow consumes this value instead of reading the clipboard directly on every action. This decouples the working input from the live clipboard, which is necessary so that after extraction the text can be edited without re-reading the original image.
- **Snapshot and restore image clipboard data.** ADR-004 currently limits restore to text. The `saved_clipboard` field becomes a `ClipboardSnapshot` enum (`Empty` | `Text(String)` | `Image { bytes, mime }`). When a synthetic copy would have destroyed the original image, the snapshot preserves it and the restore thread writes it back. This is a trust-anchor fix for the image flow.
- **Two-phase menu flow: extract, then act.** The user clicks **Extract Text**, the OCR result replaces the working selection, and the normal action grid is enabled. This gives the user a chance to review the OCR before proofreading/translating and avoids duplicating every action into an "on image" variant.
- **Resize images before sending to the model.** Large screenshots are downscaled to a configurable max dimension (default 1024 px) before base64 encoding. This bounds request size, token cost, and WebView↔Rust transfer overhead while preserving readability for OCR.
- **Platform-specific image clipboard handling.** The PAL gains `read_image` and `write_image`. The arboard path encodes/decodes RGBA via the `image` crate. The Wayland path reads and serves raw `image/png` bytes through `wl-clipboard-rs`, which is the native, persistent-serve pattern already proven on Hyprland.
- **Never log image bytes or base64 payloads.** Consistent with the existing secrets/clipboard redaction rule.

---

## Implementation Steps

### Step 1 — Extend the PAL clipboard trait for images

**Files:** `src-tauri/src/pal/mod.rs`, `src-tauri/src/pal/clipboard.rs`

- Define `ClipboardImage { pub mime: String, pub bytes: Vec<u8> }` in `pal/mod.rs`.
- Add `read_image(&mut self) -> Result<Option<ClipboardImage>, PalError>` and `write_image(&mut self, image: &ClipboardImage) -> Result<(), PalError>` to `ClipboardBackend`.
- Update `PalError::Clipboard` usage where needed; no new error variants are required.

**Outcome:** The PAL exposes a uniform image clipboard API regardless of platform.

### Step 2 — Implement image read/write in both clipboard backends

**File:** `src-tauri/src/pal/clipboard.rs`

- **ArboardClipboard:**
  - `read_image`: call `arboard::Clipboard::get_image()`. If `ContentNotAvailable`, return `Ok(None)`. Convert RGBA bytes to PNG via the `image` crate and return `ClipboardImage { mime: "image/png", bytes }`.
  - `write_image`: decode the PNG bytes to RGBA with `image`, build `arboard::ImageData { width, height, bytes }`, and call `set_image`.
- **WaylandClipboard:**
  - `read_image`: request `MimeType::Specific("image/png")` from `wl-clipboard-rs::paste::get_contents`. If the offer is empty or has no image MIME type, return `Ok(None)`. Otherwise return the bytes.
  - `write_image`: extend the existing persistent serve thread so it can serve binary data. Replace the single `String` cache with a small `ClipboardData` enum (`Text(String)`, `Image(ClipboardImage)`) and serve the appropriate bytes and MIME type. Keep the generation/ownership caching logic so self-read does not deadlock.

**Outcome:** Both backends can preserve image data across the OCR/AI loop.

### Step 3 — Add image dependencies and helper utilities

**File:** `src-tauri/Cargo.toml`

Add:

```toml
image = { version = "0.25", default-features = false, features = ["png"] }
base64 = "0.22"
```

Create a small utility module (e.g. `src-tauri/src/image_util.rs`):

- `fn to_png(rgba: &[u8], width: u32, height: u32) -> Result<Vec<u8>, String>`
- `fn resize_to_max_dimension(bytes: &[u8], max: u32) -> Result<Vec<u8>, String>` — reads the PNG, resizes maintaining aspect ratio, encodes back to PNG.
- `fn to_data_uri(image: &ClipboardImage) -> String` — `data:image/png;base64,…`.

**Outcome:** Image encoding, resizing, and base64 conversion are isolated and unit-testable.

### Step 4 — Upgrade `AppState` to hold image-capable selection and snapshot

**File:** `src-tauri/src/lib.rs`

Define the new shared state:

```rust
#[derive(Clone, Serialize)]
#[serde(tag = "kind", content = "data")]
enum SelectionContent {
    Empty,
    Text(String),
    Image(ClipboardImage),
}

#[derive(Clone)]
enum ClipboardSnapshot {
    Empty,
    Text(String),
    Image(ClipboardImage),
}
```

Update `AppState`:

```rust
struct AppState {
    pal: Mutex<Pal>,
    saved_clipboard: Mutex<ClipboardSnapshot>,
    current_input: Mutex<SelectionContent>,
    busy: Mutex<bool>,
    captions: captions::CaptionsManager,
    dictation: dictation::DictationManager,
}
```

Add a helper `restore_original_clipboard(app, snapshot)` that matches the snapshot kind and calls the correct PAL method.

**Outcome:** The daemon can preserve and restore both text and image clipboards.

### Step 5 — Update the trigger flow to detect image clipboard

**File:** `src-tauri/src/lib.rs` (`trigger_menu_flow`)

Current logic: read text, snapshot it, then simulate copy.

New logic:

1. Read text from clipboard.
2. If text is empty, read image from clipboard.
3. Snapshot the **original** clipboard content (`Text`, `Image`, or `Empty`) into `saved_clipboard`.
4. If synthetic copy is supported, simulate `Ctrl+C` (the source app may update the clipboard, but the snapshot is already saved).
5. Store the result (text or image) in `current_input`.
6. Emit `ghostpen://show` as today.

In manual mode, skip the synthetic copy and read the clipboard the user already copied into `current_input`.

**Outcome:** The menu opens with the correct input type.

### Step 6 — Upgrade the AI client for multimodal payloads

**File:** `src-tauri/src/ai.rs`

Introduce a `UserContent` enum:

```rust
pub enum UserContent {
    Text(String),
    Multimodal(Vec<Part>),
}

pub enum Part {
    Text(String),
    Image { mime: String, base64: String },
}
```

Refactor `run_completion` (and `run_completion_stream`) to accept `user: &UserContent` instead of `user: &str`. When the content is `UserContent::Text`, send the existing `"content": user` string. When it is `Multimodal`, send the OpenAI-compatible array:

```json
"content": [
  { "type": "text", "text": "Extract all text…" },
  { "type": "image_url", "image_url": { "url": "data:image/png;base64,…" } }
]
```

Keep `process_text` and other text-only callers using `UserContent::Text(...)`.

**Outcome:** One AI client can serve both text-editing and OCR requests.

### Step 7 — Add the OCR system prompt and `extract_image_text` command

**File:** `src-tauri/src/ai.rs`

Add a system prompt:

```rust
pub fn ocr_system_prompt() -> String {
    "Extract all visible text from the image. Preserve line breaks and paragraph structure as closely as possible. Return ONLY the extracted text, with no markdown, no explanations, and no wrapper quotes.".into()
}
```

**File:** `src-tauri/src/lib.rs`

Add a new Tauri command:

```rust
#[tauri::command]
async fn extract_image_text(app: AppHandle) -> Result<String, String> {
    try_acquire_busy(&app)?;
    let result = extract_image_text_inner(&app).await;
    release_busy(&app);
    result
}

async fn extract_image_text_inner(app: &AppHandle) -> Result<String, String> {
    let settings = load_settings(app);
    let mut profile = settings.active().ok_or("No active AI profile configured")?.clone();
    // Optional: allow a per-profile or settings OCR model override.

    let image = {
        let state = app.state::<AppState>();
        let input = lock_recover(&state.current_input);
        match input.clone() {
            SelectionContent::Image(img) => img,
            _ => return Err("No image on the clipboard".into()),
        }
    };

    let resized = image_util::resize_to_max_dimension(&image.bytes, settings.ocr.max_dimension)
        .map_err(|e| format!("Image resize failed: {e}"))?;
    let data_uri = image_util::to_data_uri(&ClipboardImage { mime: image.mime, bytes: resized });

    let content = ai::UserContent::Multimodal(vec![
        ai::Part::Text("Extract all text from this image.".into()),
        ai::Part::Image { mime: "image/png".into(), base64: data_uri },
    ]);

    let text = ai::run_completion(&profile, &ai::ocr_system_prompt(), &content).await?;

    // Replace the working input with the extracted text so normal actions can run on it.
    {
        let state = app.state::<AppState>();
        *lock_recover(&state.current_input) = SelectionContent::Text(text.clone());
    }

    Ok(text)
}
```

Register `extract_image_text` in the `invoke_handler`.

**Outcome:** Backend can OCR an image and make the result the new working selection.

### Step 8 — Refactor `process_inner` to consume `SelectionContent`

**File:** `src-tauri/src/lib.rs`

Change `process_inner` to read from `current_input` instead of the clipboard directly:

```rust
let selected = {
    let state = app.state::<AppState>();
    let input = lock_recover(&state.current_input);
    match input.clone() {
        SelectionContent::Text(s) => s,
        SelectionContent::Image(_) => {
            return Err("Extract text from the image first".into())
        }
        SelectionContent::Empty => {
            return Err("No text selected".into())
        }
    }
};
```

The rest of the write/hide/paste/restore logic stays the same, but `restore_original_clipboard` must handle the image snapshot.

Update `process_ai_action` and `process_ai_custom` to behave the same way; if `current_input` is an image, they return the same "Extract text first" message.

**Outcome:** Existing actions work on extracted text and do not accidentally try to send an image to a text-only prompt.

### Step 9 — Add OCR settings

**File:** `src-tauri/src/config.rs`

Add:

```rust
#[derive(Clone, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct OcrSettings {
    #[serde(default = "default_ocr_max_dim", rename = "maxDimension")]
    pub max_dimension: u32,
    #[serde(default = "default_ocr_prompt", rename = "systemPrompt")]
    pub system_prompt: String,
    /// Optional model override; empty uses the active profile.
    #[serde(default, rename = "modelOverride")]
    pub model_override: String,
}

fn default_ocr_max_dim() -> u32 { 1024 }
fn default_ocr_prompt() -> String { ai::ocr_system_prompt() }

impl Default for OcrSettings {
    fn default() -> Self { ... }
}
```

Add `ocr: OcrSettings` to `Settings` with `#[serde(default)]`.

**Outcome:** Users can tune image size and prompt without code changes.

### Step 10 — Update frontend types and API

**File:** `src/api.ts`

Add:

```ts
export interface ClipboardImage {
  mime: string;
  base64: string;
}

export type SelectionContent =
  | { kind: "empty" }
  | { kind: "text"; data: string }
  | { kind: "image"; data: ClipboardImage };

export interface OcrSettings {
  maxDimension: number;
  systemPrompt: string;
  modelOverride: string;
}
```

Change `getSelection` to return `SelectionContent` and add:

```ts
export const extractImageText = () => invoke<string>("extract_image_text");
```

Update the `Settings` interface to include `ocr: OcrSettings`.

**Outcome:** TypeScript mirrors the new Rust DTOs.

### Step 11 — Update `Menu.tsx` to render image preview and extract affordance

**File:** `src/Menu.tsx`

- Change `selection` state from `string` to `SelectionContent`.
- In the `refresh` callback, call `getSelection()` and store the full content.
- In the menu view:
  - If `kind === "image"`, render an `<img src={data.base64} />` preview with a small thumbnail style.
  - If `kind === "empty"`, show the existing empty hint.
  - If `kind === "text"`, show the existing text preview.
- Add a new built-in menu item **"Extract Text"** (icon: `scan` or `ocr`) when the current content is an image.
  - Disable normal action buttons when an image is present (except Extract Text).
  - Clicking Extract Text calls `extractImageText()`, shows a loading state, then updates `selection` to `Text` and re-enables the normal actions.
- Add a keyboard shortcut for Extract Text (e.g. `e` or `Enter` when the image is focused).

**Outcome:** The frontend supports the image → OCR → text flow.

### Step 12 — Add OCR settings panel

**File:** `src/Settings.tsx`

Add a card **"Image Text Extraction (OCR)"** after the Custom Actions section:

- Max dimension slider/input (e.g. 512–2048).
- System prompt textarea (default shown, editable).
- Model override input (optional, empty = active profile).
- A short note: "OCR uses your active AI profile. The model must support vision (e.g. gemma4:e4b)."

Wire the `ocr` object through `update`/`updateOcr` helpers.

**Outcome:** OCR behavior is user-configurable.

### Step 13 — Update capabilities and window configuration if needed

**Files:** `src-tauri/capabilities/default.json`, `src-tauri/tauri.conf.json`

No new permissions should be required for this feature (it uses existing clipboard/Tauri commands). If the implementer adds a temporary file path for image data, ensure the file path is read from the Rust backend, not from the frontend directly.

**Outcome:** The feature does not widen the Tauri capability surface.

### Step 14 — Tests and verification

**Files:** `src-tauri/src/lib.rs` (tests), new unit tests for image utilities

- Add unit tests for `image_util::to_png`, `resize_to_max_dimension`, and `to_data_uri`.
- Add a Rust unit test for `SelectionContent` JSON serialization.
- Add a manual test checklist:
  1. Copy text → trigger → menu shows text; existing actions still work.
  2. Copy an image → trigger → menu shows image preview; **Extract Text** is available.
  3. Click Extract Text → OCR text appears; run Proofread → result is pasted.
  4. After final paste, the original image is restored to the clipboard.
  5. Configure a text-only model (e.g. a small local model) → Extract Text fails with a readable error.
  6. On Wayland/Hyprland: image read, preview, and restore all work.
  7. `cargo check`, `cargo test`, `npm run build` pass.

**Outcome:** The feature is verified and regressions are caught.

---

## Risk Assessment

| Risk | Likelihood | Impact | Mitigation |
|------|-----------|--------|-----------|
| **Wayland image serving** via `wl-clipboard-rs` is not as straightforward as text serving; MIME handling and binary serve may require iteration. | Medium | High | Spike the `read_image`/`write_image` paths on the Hyprland target before wiring the full menu flow. Fall back to manual-copy if restore fails. |
| **Arboard image write** on Windows/macOS may have format or handle quirks. | Medium | Medium | Test copy→trigger→restore on each target; encode/decode RGBA through the `image` crate. |
| **Heavy `image` crate dependency** increases build time/binary size. | Medium | Low | Use `default-features = false, features = ["png"]` to limit to PNG only. |
| **Vision model not configured** or endpoint returns a non-obvious error. | Medium | High | Surface a clear UI message: "The active model does not support images. Try a vision model like gemma4:e4b." |
| **Large base64 payloads** between Rust and WebView cause memory spikes or UI lag. | Medium | Medium | Resize to a max dimension before transfer; never send raw full-resolution screenshots. |
| **Restoring original image clipboard races** with the target app consuming the pasted text. | Low | Medium | Keep the configurable restore delay and apply it to image snapshots as well. |
| **Privacy/clipboard logging** — image bytes or base64 could be logged by accident. | Low | High | Explicitly never log `bytes`/`base64`; log only dimensions and MIME type. |

---

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Clipboard with text: unchanged behavior; menu shows text and actions work.
- [ ] Clipboard with image: menu shows a preview and the **Extract Text** action.
- [ ] **Extract Text** calls the active vision model and returns the recognized text into the menu.
- [ ] After extraction, the normal actions (proofread, translate, etc.) operate on the extracted text.
- [ ] After the final paste, the **original image** is restored to the clipboard.
- [ ] Manual-copy mode works: user copies the image, triggers the menu, extracts text, and pastes the result manually.
- [ ] Text-only model configured → readable error, no crash, original clipboard preserved.
- [ ] Settings panel exposes OCR max dimension, prompt, and optional model override.
- [ ] `cargo check`, `cargo test`, and `npm run build` pass without new warnings.
- [ ] Manual verification performed on the primary Wayland/Hyprland target and at least one other platform.

---
